steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: A Linode is your own apartment in Akamai's building. Fully isolated from neighbours. You pick the size, the layout, the locks on the door.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
A Linode is a virtual machine running on Akamai Connected Cloud — what AWS calls EC2 and GCP calls Compute Engine. You control:

- Plan size (Nanode, Shared, Dedicated, GPU)
- Region (one of 30+ data centers worldwide)
- OS image (Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, Alpine, custom)
- Networking (private IP, VLAN, firewall rules)

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Spin one up with linode-cli linodes create. Production teams provision them via Terraform or the Linode API — never the dashboard. A g6-dedicated-4 (4 vCPU, 8GB RAM) costs ~$36/month flat. No hidden egress fees.
"A Linode is a VM you actually own — flat pricing, no surprises, in 30+ regions worldwide."

quiz.question: What's the main pricing difference between Linodes and AWS EC2?

options.explain: Both bill hourly. The real difference is egress: Linode includes generous bandwidth, AWS charges per GB.

options.text: Linode pricing includes generous outbound bandwidth
options.explain: Linode plans include 1–20 TB of outbound transfer. AWS charges $0.09/GB after the first GB.
Linodes are on-demand with flat hourly pricing — no spot model.
Linodes resize between plans with a single API call or CLI command.

quiz.question: You need a GPU for ML inference. What do you provision?

options.explain: Dedicated CPU has no GPU. You need the GPU plan family.

options.text: A GPU Linode (RTX 4000 Ada / NVIDIA Quadro)
options.explain: Linode's GPU plans expose RTX 4000 Ada and Quadro RTX 6000 cards with NVIDIA drivers pre-installed.
You can add GPU nodes to LKE, but the underlying instance type is still a GPU Linode.
Nanodes are the smallest, cheapest, no-GPU tier.
